free_text: Highly accurate spreading width G' of isobaric
analog states in 139La from 138Ba(p,p') using a
two-level S-matrix resonance parametrization
HVEC FN tandem Van de Graaff accelerator
Lamb-shift source LASCO
Isotopically enriched targets (purity >99%, supported
by 10 mu-g/cm2 carbon foils) with a barium thickness of
53 mu-g/cm2 for the first beam time and 151 mu-g/cm2
for the other two beam times.
Eight Si surface barrier detectors were mounted
symmetrically to the left and right of the beam
Two further detectors were mounted at 45. degr. as
monitors for target thickness determination via
Rutherford scattering at Elab = 7 MeV.
The beam polarization was monitored simultaneously
with a 4He polarimeter with two detectors at
Angle(Lab) = 112. installed in the Faraday cup behind
the scattering chamber.
Additionally, data set cross section * analyzing power
are given on figs 4,5(b).
No information about source of uncertainty.
SD
figs 2,3 from Nucl.Phys.,A501(1989)513
figs 2,3 from Nucl.Phys.,A501(1989)513
The first 2+ collective state at an excitation energy
E = 1.436 MeV
figs 4,5 from Nucl.Phys.,A501(1989)513
